claim: Attention addresses the fixed-vector bottleneck in early encoder-decoder NMT by soft-searching relevant source positions during decoding.
RNN Encoder-Decoder models map variable input sequences to target sequences and learned meaningful phrase representations for SMT/NMT.
Transformers replaced recurrence/convolution with attention-only architecture and improved parallelization/training time for translation tasks.
verified_with: GNMT paper as prior recurrent production system https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.08144

claim: BPE/subword units help NMT handle rare and unknown words in fixed-vocabulary systems.
SentencePiece supports language-independent tokenization/detokenization from raw sentences without requiring pre-tokenization.
Speculative decoding drafts multiple future tokens with a smaller model and verifies with the large model, preserving output distribution when exact.
Non-autoregressive NMT can lower latency by producing output tokens in parallel but may trade off quality versus autoregressive teachers.
Medusa adds multiple decoding heads and verifies candidate continuations with tree attention to reduce decoding steps.
Self-Refine improves outputs through generate-feedback-refine loops without weight updates.
Reflexion stores verbal reflections from task feedback in episodic memory rather than updating weights.
ReAct interleaves reasoning traces and external actions to reduce hallucination/error propagation in tool environments.
